payload.subject: Opus remediation COMPLETE — v37 held-out_r=0.639 (−0.041 vs v35, p=0.617, not significant) (turn 27)
payload.summary: Gate resolved. All 999 Opus-scored texts re-scored with Sonnet-4-6 across all 10 dimensions (separated-LLM protocol). v37 trained on clean Sonnet-only foundation and evaluated on held-out set. held-out_r=0.639 vs v35 baseline 0.680. Statistical comparison: z=0.50, p=0.617 — not significant at n=100 (SE(r)≈0.10). Regression is within the noise floor. Notable but non-significant: CC (−0.109) and CO (−0.106) show largest individual decrements.

remediation_completion.protocol: separated-LLM (one dimension per scoring session, halo-elimination)
remediation_completion.opus_scores_status: preserved in DB but deprioritized — best_scores view priority is Sonnet > Opus. Opus scores not deleted per preservation-of-audit-trail policy.

v37_training.vs_v35_change: 999 Opus-scored training texts replaced with Sonnet labels. Same architecture, same hyperparameters.

statistical_test.interpretation: Not significant. SE(r)≈0.10 at n=100 means the 95% CI for the difference spans ±0.196 Fisher-z units. The observed difference (0.041) is less than half a standard error.

held_out_evaluation.regression_interpretation: 9/10 dims show numerical decrease; 1/10 (energy_dissipation) improved. No dim reaches p<0.05. Consistent with random variation at n=100 rather than systematic Sonnet-label degradation. CC and CO are flagged — see epistemic notes.

spot_check_note.feasibility: NOT FEASIBLE from current DB state
spot_check_note.reason: The rescore-368 texts had their original Sonnet scores deleted during the revert step (2026-03-06), then Opus-scored, then Sonnet re-scored in this remediation. No overlapping Sonnet sessions exist. DB confirms: 9,990 scores = exactly 1 per text-dimension cell (zero duplicates). Recovering original Sonnet labels would require a separate re-scoring pass. Low priority per turn 20 guidance — leaving as a future option.

immediate: ONNX export (fp32 + INT8 quantized) from models/psq-v37/best.pt
Deploy v37 to Hetzner endpoint (replacing v35)
next_steps.after_psychology_agent_review: Bifactor modeling (g + bipolar + DA + CO specification per turn 25) ready to begin.

claims.text: All 999 Opus-scored texts have been re-scored with Sonnet-4-6 across all 10 PSQ dimensions using the separated-LLM protocol. 9,990 new Sonnet scores are in psq.db with no duplicate cells.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct verification: label_separated.py assemble produced 999-text JSONL (10,000 dim-scores minus 10 missing = 9,990 complete); migrate.py ingest confirmed; DB query shows exactly 9,990 Sonnet scores for the 999 remediation text IDs with 0 cells having >1 score.

claims.text: v37 held-out_r = 0.6391. The −0.041 regression vs v35 (0.680) is not statistically significant (Fisher z=0.50, p=0.617, n=100). No individual dimension reaches p<0.05.
claims.confidence_basis: eval_held_out.py run on models/psq-v37/best.pt against held-out-test.jsonl (100 held-out texts, real-world labels). Fisher z-test applied per standard formula for independent samples sharing the same criterion. SE(r)≈0.10 is an established noise ceiling for n=100 Pearson r.

claims.text: The CC and CO regressions (−0.109, −0.106) are large enough to warrant epistemic flagging but are not statistically significant at n=100 (p=0.160, p=0.333). They may reflect genuine scorer inconsistency for these dimensions rather than systematic degradation.
claims.confidence_basis: Both CC and CO had the weakest Opus-Sonnet concordance in the earlier study (CC ICC=0.624, CO ICC=0.491). Whether Sonnet-only training helps or hurts CC/CO held-out prediction cannot be resolved at n=100. A 5-fold cross-validated comparison on the full training set would be more informative but was not run.

action_gate.gate_note: Opus remediation gate resolved per turn 20 condition (Sonnet re-score + v37 retrain + held-out metrics). PSQ sub-agent will proceed with ONNX export and Hetzner deployment. Bifactor modeling available to begin on psychology-agent's signal.

epistemic_flags: n=100 held-out set has SE(r)≈0.10. At this sample size, differences up to ±0.20 are within 2σ. The −0.041 overall regression and the individual CC/CO regressions (−0.109, −0.106) are all within this range. Held-out evaluation at n=100 is a necessary but not sufficient basis for model ranking; the ranking between v35 and v37 is statistically unresolved.

CC and CO both had low Opus-Sonnet concordance in the earlier study (CC ICC=0.624, CO ICC=0.491). If those dimensions are genuinely noisier under Sonnet scoring, then v37's lower CC/CO held-out r might reflect that the held-out labels (Sonnet) are now less predictable from text features alone — not that v37 is worse at learning. Disambiguating requires either larger held-out n or a controlled A/B retraining study.

Sonnet-Sonnet test-retest for the remediation texts was not done (original labels were deleted during the revert step). The assumption that Sonnet scores are consistent for these 999 texts rests on the concordance study's positive RC ICC (0.755) and the overall Sonnet baseline used in all prior validation.

TE test_r = 0.068 in the training evaluation is anomalously low vs held-out TE r = 0.754. This suggests the test split for TE has a different distribution than the held-out set. Not a concern for production inference but flags that the training evaluation understates TE model quality.
